quotes.text: I am the man who accompanied Jacqueline Kennedy to Paris, and I have enjoyed it.
quotes.speaker: John F. Kennedy
quotes.context: Self-deprecating opening line at a press luncheon at the Palais de Chaillot in Paris on June 2, 1961, during a state visit to France.

source.title: Remarks and Question and Answer Period at the Press Luncheon in Paris | The American Presidency Project

quotes.text: I've been in enough budget meetings to know that the phrase 'we'll circle back on that' is Washington's most expensive sentence.
quotes.speaker: Mitch McConnell
quotes.context: Remarks at a Kentucky business roundtable, 2023
quotes.fake_note: McConnell is known for dry, procedural humor about Washington dysfunction, and budget process jokes fit his Senate Finance background perfectly. The subtle giveaway is that McConnell rarely offers self-deprecating institutional jokes this quotable — his wit tends to be more guarded.

quotes.text: I've now been in 57 states? I think one left to go.
quotes.speaker: Barack Obama
quotes.context: Then a Democratic U.S. Senator from Illinois campaigning for president, Obama misspoke at a May 9, 2008 event in Beaverton, Oregon, after a long stretch of travel (he meant 47).

quotes.text: My grandmother used to say you can tell everything about a person by how they treat a waiter and how they handle a lost luggage claim. She was right on both counts.
quotes.speaker: Pete Buttigieg
quotes.context: Interview with a midwest regional newspaper, 2024
quotes.fake_note: Buttigieg frequently invokes his grandmother and Midwestern values, and transportation-adjacent anecdotes fit his portfolio perfectly. The giveaway is that his real grandmother references tend to be more substantive and policy-linked rather than this tidy a parable.

quotes.text: I will say this about C-SPAN — it is the only place on television where falling asleep is considered a sign of respect for the process.
quotes.speaker: Nancy Pelosi
quotes.context: Remarks at a Democratic fundraiser dinner, Washington D.C., 2022
quotes.fake_note: Pelosi has a well-documented dry wit and is known to make self-aware jokes about Congress's public image. The subtle tell is that Pelosi rarely punches at the institution she spent decades leading — her real humor tends to be sharper and more directional.

quotes.speaker: George W. Bush
quotes.context: George W. Bush, then the sitting Republican Governor of Texas and a presidential candidate, coined this signature malapropism on Nov. 6, 2000 in Bentonville, Arkansas. A classic, self-aimed, innocuous Bushism with no target or controversy.
